essence: Protects public health by inspecting environments, enforcing regulations, and managing environmental risks.

identity.title: Environmental Health Officer
identity.summary: Investigates environmental hazards, inspects businesses, enforces public‑health regulations, and ensures safe living and working conditions.

salary.notes: Senior EHOs or specialists in food safety, housing, or environmental protection can exceed £65k–£80k+.

qualifications: Degree or training in environmental health
Knowledge of public‑health legislation and enforcement
Ability to conduct inspections and investigations
Understanding of food safety, housing standards, and environmental hazards

lifestyle.description: Environmental health officers work for councils, public‑health bodies, and regulatory agencies. They inspect food businesses, housing, workplaces, and public spaces to ensure safety and compliance. The role suits individuals who enjoy investigation, regulation, and protecting community wellbeing.

habits: Writes clear reports and legal notices
Communicates with businesses and the public
